message:
chore: commit accumulated changes from parallel agent work

Includes updates to:
- cells.rs: cell width calculations
- console.rs: console improvements
- markup/mod.rs: markup parser enhancements
- measure.rs: measurement protocol updates
- padding.rs: padding behavior
- tree.rs: tree rendering fixes
- segment.rs: segment operations
- style.rs: style system updates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"rich_rust-kko","title":"Validate Style System against RICH_SPEC Section 2","description":"## Objective
Verify the Style implementation in src/style.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 2 exactly.

## Background
The Style system is fundamental to rich_rust - it encapsulates visual attributes (colors, bold, italic, etc.) that are applied to text. The RICH_SPEC defines 13 attributes using bitflags and specific combine/override semantics.

## Validation Checklist

### 2.1 Attributes Bitflags
- [ ] Verify all 13 attributes defined: BOLD, DIM, ITALIC, UNDERLINE, BLINK, BLINK2, REVERSE, CONCEAL, STRIKE, UNDERLINE2, FRAME, ENCIRCLE, OVERLINE
- [ ] Verify bit positions match spec (BOLD=1, DIM=2, ITALIC=4, etc.)
- [ ] Verify set() and unset() methods work correctly
- [ ] Verify is_set() method returns correct boolean

### 2.2 Style Struct
- [ ] Verify fields: color (Option<Color>), bgcolor (Option<Color>), attributes (Attributes), link (Option<String>), meta (Option<HashMap>)
- [ ] Verify Style::null() creates empty style (all None, empty attributes)
- [ ] Verify Style::new() creates null style
- [ ] Verify builder methods: bold(), italic(), underline(), strike(), dim(), reverse(), blink(), conceal(), overline()
- [ ] Verify color() and on() methods for foreground/background colors
- [ ] Verify link() method for hyperlinks (OSC 8 support)

### 2.3 Style Combination
- [ ] Verify combine(&other) merges styles with other taking precedence
- [ ] Test: null.combine(bold) = bold
- [ ] Test: bold.combine(italic) = bold+italic
- [ ] Test: red.combine(blue) = blue (color override)
- [ ] Verify attribute union: self.attributes | other.attributes

### 2.4 Style Parsing
- [ ] Verify Style::parse() handles named colors
- [ ] Verify attribute keywords: bold, dim, italic, underline, blink, reverse, conceal, strike, overline
- [ ] Verify 'on' keyword for background
- [ ] Verify 'not' prefix for unsetting
- [ ] Verify 'link=URL' syntax for hyperlinks

### 2.5 ANSI Code Generation
- [ ] Verify render_to_ansi() produces correct SGR sequences
- [ ] Verify color system downgrade: TrueColor->EightBit->Standard
- [ ] Verify reset code (0) when style is null

## Files to Examine
- src/style.rs (primary)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 2","acceptance_criteria":"All checklist items verified and documented. Any discrepancies logged as separate bug/task beads.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:16:40.607570332-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:39:37.493238226-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:39:37.493238226-05:00","close_reason":"Validation complete. - 2.2 Aliases: All parsing aliases match (b,d,i,u,r,c,s,uu,o)
- 2.3 Style Parsing: All formats supported (empty, attr, not, color, on, link, combined)
- 2.4 Combination: Logic matches spec exactly
- 2.7 StyleStack: Implementation matches with defensive pop() guard

## Minor Deviations Found (issues created):
- rich_rust-pax: Missing link_id field for hyperlink tracking (P3)
- rich_rust-9l7: Missing meta field for serialized metadata (P4)

 {"id":"rich_rust-ms5","title":"Verify Table Width Collapse Algorithm","description":"## Objective
Verify collapse_widths() in Table matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 9 exactly.

## Background
When table content exceeds available width, collapse_widths() must reduce column widths proportionally while respecting minimum widths. This is a complex algorithm with many edge cases.

## Technical Details

### Spec Requirement (Section 9.4)
collapse_widths() should:
1. Calculate excess = current_total - available_width
2. Distribute reduction proportionally to (current - minimum)
3. Never reduce below minimum width
4. Handle columns that hit minimum (redistribute to others)

### Algorithm Verification
```
Given: 3 columns with widths [40, 30, 30] = 100
       minimums [10, 10, 10]
       available width = 70
       excess = 30

Step 1: Calculate reducible space per column
  col0: 40-10 = 30 reducible
  col1: 30-10 = 20 reducible
  col2: 30-10 = 20 reducible
  total reducible = 70

Step 2: Distribute reduction proportionally
  col0 reduces by: 30 * (30/70) = ~12.9
  col1 reduces by: 30 * (20/70) = ~8.6
  col2 reduces by: 30 * (20/70) = ~8.6

Result: [27, 21, 22] (with rounding)
```

### Test Cases
1. All columns have same reducible space
2. One column at minimum (can't reduce further)
3. All columns at minimum
4. Single column table
5. Very narrow width (forces all to minimum)

## Files to Examine
- src/renderables/table.rs (collapse_widths function)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.4","acceptance_criteria":"collapse_widths matches spec. All test cases pass.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:21:58.81508283-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:37:04.558811593-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:37:04.558811593-05:00","close_reason":"Validated: collapse_widths() now matches RICH_SPEC Section 9.3 exactly. Includes:
- Proportional reduction based on (current - minimum) ratios
- Post-loop rounding correction (spec lines 1680-1694)
Tests pass. Implementation at table.rs:646-698.","labels":["critical","implementation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-ms5","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:23:03.781926826-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-ms5","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q5f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:46:58.046952534-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
-{"id":"rich_rust-mst","title":"Validate Box Characters against RICH_SPEC Section 8","description":"## Objective
Verify the Box implementation in src/box.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 8 exactly.

## Background
Box characters are used to draw borders around tables and panels. The spec defines 8 character positions per row and 8 row types, plus built-in styles (ASCII, ROUNDED, DOUBLE, etc.).

## Validation Checklist

### 8.1 BoxChars Struct
- [ ] Verify 8 row types: top, head, head_row, mid, row, foot_row, foot, bottom
- [ ] Verify each row has: left, left_divider, divider, right_divider, right, cross, vertical, horizontal (8 chars)
- [ ] Total: 64 character positions

### 8.2 Built-in Box Styles
- [ ] Verify ASCII box: +, -, |, etc.
- [ ] Verify ASCII2 box: variations
- [ ] Verify ASCII_DOUBLE_HEAD: double top line
- [ ] Verify ROUNDED box: rounded corners (╭╮╰╯)
- [ ] Verify SQUARE box: sharp corners (┌┐└┘)
- [ ] Verify DOUBLE box: double lines (═║╔╗╚╝)
- [ ] Verify HEAVY box: heavy lines (━┃┏┓┗┛)
- [ ] Verify HEAVY_HEAD: heavy top only
- [ ] Verify MINIMAL box: minimal borders
- [ ] Verify SIMPLE box: simple lines
- [ ] Verify SIMPLE_HEAVY: simple with heavy lines

### 8.3 Box Methods
- [ ] Verify get_top() returns top row chars
- [ ] Verify get_row() returns content row chars
- [ ] Verify get_bottom() returns bottom row chars
- [ ] Verify substitute(safe_box) for ASCII fallback

### 8.4 Safe Box Mode
- [ ] Verify safe_box=true falls back to ASCII
- [ ] Verify non-ASCII terminals get ASCII automatically
- [ ] Verify detection of Unicode support

## Files to Examine
- src/box.rs (primary)

 {"id":"rich_rust-nqh","title":"Create Migration Guide from Python Rich","description":"## Objective
Create a migration guide helping Python Rich users adopt rich_rust.

## Guide Contents

### Feature Mapping Table
| Python Rich | rich_rust | Notes |
|-------------|-----------|-------|
| console.print() | Console::print() | Same semantics |
| Text() | Text::new() | Similar API |
| Style() | Style::new() | Builder pattern |
| Table() | Table::new() | Similar API |
| Panel() | Panel::new() | Similar API |
| Markdown() | Markdown::parse() | Feature flag |
| Syntax() | Syntax::new() | Feature flag |
| Live() | Not implemented | Excluded |
| Status() | Not implemented | Excluded |

### API Differences
- Python: console.print(text, style='bold')
- Rust: console.print('[bold]text[/]');

### Markup Syntax
- Same: [bold], [red], [on blue], [/]
- Same: Escaping with backslash

### Console Options
- force_terminal -> force_terminal
- record -> record
- width -> width()
- color_system -> color_system()

### Common Patterns

Python:
```python
console = Console()
table = Table(title='My Table')
table.add_column('Name')
table.add_row('Alice')
console.print(table)
```

Rust:
```rust
let console = Console::new();
let table = Table::new()
    .title('My Table')
    .add_column(Column::new('Name'))
    .add_row(Row::new(vec\![Cell::new('Alice')]));
console.print(table);
```

### Feature Flags
- syntax: Syntax highlighting (requires syntect)
- markdown: Markdown rendering (requires pulldown-cmark)
- json: JSON rendering (requires serde_json)

## Files to Create
- docs/MIGRATION.md","acceptance_criteria":"Migration guide covers all shared features. Code examples work.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:37.84434352-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:37.84434352-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-nqh","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-2oy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:48.024395587-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-p8z","title":"Validate Segment Operations against RICH_SPEC Section 3","description":"## Objective
Verify the Segment implementation in src/segment.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 3 exactly.

## Background
Segment is the atomic rendering unit - a piece of text with a single style. The rendering pipeline produces streams of segments. Control segments handle terminal manipulation (cursor, clear, etc.).

## Validation Checklist

### 3.1 Segment Struct
- [ ] Verify fields: text (String), style (Option<Style>), control (Option<Vec<ControlCode>>)
- [ ] Verify Segment::new(text, style) constructor
- [ ] Verify Segment::styled(text, style) convenience constructor
- [ ] Verify Segment::plain(text) for unstyled text
- [ ] Verify Segment::line() creates newline segment
- [ ] Verify Segment::control(codes) creates control segment

### 3.2 ControlType Enum
- [ ] Verify all control types: Bell, CarriageReturn, Home, Clear, ShowCursor, HideCursor, EnableAltScreen, DisableAltScreen, CursorUp, CursorDown, CursorForward, CursorBackward, CursorMoveToColumn, CursorMoveTo, EraseInLine, SetWindowTitle
- [ ] Verify ControlCode struct with control_type and params fields

### 3.3 Segment Methods
- [ ] Verify is_control() returns true iff control is Some
- [ ] Verify cell_length() returns 0 for control segments
- [ ] Verify cell_length() uses Unicode cell width for text
- [ ] Verify is_empty() checks both text and control
- [ ] Verify split_at_cell(pos) splits at cell position (not byte)

### 3.4 Segment Operations (Functions)
- [ ] Verify apply_style(segments, style, post_style)
- [ ] Verify split_lines(segments) splits at newlines
- [ ] Verify adjust_line_length(line, length, style, pad)
- [ ] Verify simplify(segments) merges adjacent same-style
- [ ] Verify divide(segments, cuts) divides at cell positions
- [ ] Verify align_top/bottom/middle vertical alignment

### 3.5 Edge Cases
- [ ] Test split_at_cell with wide characters (CJK, emoji)
- [ ] Test divide with cuts at wide character boundaries
- [ ] Test simplify preserves control segments

## Files to Examine
- src/segment.rs (primary)
- src/cells.rs (cell width calculation)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 3","acceptance_criteria":"All checklist items verified. Edge case handling verified.","notes":"Validation complete vs RICH_SPEC §3: ControlType list, Segment struct, and operations (apply_style, split_lines, adjust_line_length, simplify, divide, align_*), plus tests in src/segment.rs, align with spec. No code changes needed; blocked only by epic rich_rust-q1k closure.","status":"blocked","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:16:52.788032827-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T20:11:09.917712998-05:00","labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-p8z","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:35.749658257-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-pax","title":"Style missing link_id field for hyperlink tracking","description":"## Issue

Per RICH_SPEC.md Section 2.6, the Style struct should have a `link_id: String` field for hyperlink tracking in OSC 8 sequences.

Current implementation:
- `Style` struct is missing the `link_id` field
- Hyperlink output uses empty id: `\x1b]8;;{url}\x1b\\`
- Should be: `\x1b]8;id={link_id};{url}\x1b\\`

## Impact
Low - hyperlinks work without ids, but tracking/deduplication is not possible.

## Fix
1. Add `link_id: Option<String>` to Style struct
2. Generate unique id when link is set (if not provided)
3. Update render() and render_ansi() to include id in OSC 8 sequence","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T23:38:59.071153066-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:39:13.84549087-05:00","labels":["implementation","style"]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-q1k","title":"EPIC: Spec Validation & Implementation Alignment","description":"# EPIC: Spec Validation & Implementation Alignment

## Overview
Comprehensive validation of the Rust implementation against RICH_SPEC.md to ensure behavioral parity with Python Rich library.

## Background & Reasoning
The rich_rust project is a port of Python's Rich library to Rust. The RICH_SPEC.md document contains the complete behavioral specification extracted from Python Rich source (v13.x). This epic ensures:

1. **Behavioral Correctness**: Every feature works exactly as specified
2. **Edge Case Coverage**: Boundary conditions handled correctly  
3. **Algorithm Fidelity**: Complex algorithms (color conversion, ratio distribution, text division) match spec
4. **API Completeness**: All documented methods and properties exist

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All spec sections validated against implementation
- [ ] Discrepancies documented and resolved
- [ ] Test coverage for each validated behavior
- [ ] No regressions in existing functionality

## Child Tasks (by module)
1. Color System validation
2. Style System validation
3. Segment operations validation
4. Markup parser validation
5. Measurement protocol validation
6. Text object validation (CRITICAL: division algorithm)
7. Console validation
8. Box characters validation
9. Table layout validation (CRITICAL: width calculation)
10. Panel & Padding validation
11. Alignment system validation
12. Unicode cell width validation
13. Text wrapping validation
14. Ratio distribution validation

## Dependencies
- Blocks: All implementation refinement tasks
- Blocks: All performance optimization tasks

## Technical Notes
- Use the RICH_SPEC.md as the source of truth
- Create property-based tests where applicable
- Document any intentional deviations from spec","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:13:29.484048364-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T11:25:35.812552966-05:00"}
 {"id":"rich_rust-q5f","title":"Validate Table Width Algorithms against RICH_SPEC Section 9.4","description":"## Objective
Verify Table width calculation algorithms match RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.4 EXACTLY.

## Background
This is the CRITICAL algorithm validation for Table. Width calculation is complex and must match spec precisely to avoid layout bugs.

## Validation Checklist

### get_row_widths()
- [ ] Measures each cell in row
- [ ] Returns Vec<Measurement> with min/max per column
- [ ] Accounts for padding
- [ ] Handles multi-line cells correctly

### calculate_widths() Main Algorithm
- [ ] Step 1: Get minimum and maximum widths per column
- [ ] Step 2: Calculate total minimum width
- [ ] Step 3: If total minimum > available: use minimums (clipped)
- [ ] Step 4: If total maximum <= available AND expand: use maximums
- [ ] Step 5: If total minimum < available < total maximum: distribute
- [ ] Step 6: Account for borders and padding in available width

### expand_widths() - Distributing Extra Space
- [ ] Calculate extra = available - current_total
- [ ] Distribute by ratio (ratio_distribute algorithm)
- [ ] Columns without ratio get default ratio = 1
- [ ] Columns with ratio = 0 get no extra space
- [ ] Verify: sum of distributed = extra exactly (no rounding loss)

### collapse_widths() - Reducing Widths
- [ ] Calculate excess = current_total - available
- [ ] Calculate reducible space per column = current - minimum
- [ ] Distribute reduction proportionally to reducible space
- [ ] Never go below minimum
- [ ] When column hits minimum, redistribute to others
- [ ] Verify: sum of reductions = excess exactly

## Test Vectors (from spec)
```
Test 1: expand_widths
  Input:  widths=[20,20,20], ratios=[1,2,1], available=100
  Expect: [30,40,30]  (extra 40 distributed 1:2:1)

Test 2: collapse_widths
  Input:  widths=[40,30,30], minimums=[10,10,10], available=70
  Expect: ~[27,21,22] (excess 30 reduced proportionally)

Test 3: boundary
  Input:  widths=[10,10,10], minimums=[10,10,10], available=20
  Expect: [10,10,10] (can't go below minimum, clipped)
```

## Logging Requirements
When running tests, log:
- Input widths, minimums, maximums
- Available width
- Each step of the algorithm
- Final output widths
- Verification that sum matches

## Files to Examine
- src/renderables/table.rs (width calculation functions)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.4","acceptance_criteria":"Width algorithms match spec EXACTLY. All test vectors pass. Logs show calculation steps.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:46:41.664745764-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T13:14:47.104456048-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T13:14:47.104456048-05:00","close_reason":"## Validation Complete

### Findings Summary

1. **calculate_widths()** - CORRECT: Main algorithm follows spec structure

2. **expand_widths()** - CRITICAL DISCREPANCY (rich_rust-t54):
   - Distributes extra space equally instead of by column ratio
   - Column.ratio field is completely ignored
   
3. **collapse_widths()** - MINOR DISCREPANCY (rich_rust-gk4):
   - Missing rounding error correction loop from spec
   - May cause off-by-one in edge cases

### Deliverables
- Created tests/e2e_width_algorithms.rs with test vectors
- Created bug issues: rich_rust-t54 (P1), rich_rust-gk4 (P2)
- All tests pass

### Test Vector Evidence
- expand_widths: widths=[20,20,20], ratios=[1,2,1], available=100
  - Expected: [30,40,30], Actual: [33,33,34]","labels":["phase-1","validation"]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-qgs","title":"Validate Markup Parser against RICH_SPEC Section 4","description":"## Objective
Verify the Markup parser in src/markup/mod.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 4 exactly.

## Background
The markup parser converts Rich-style markup like '[bold red]Hello[/]' into styled segments. It's the primary user-facing API for styling text. The parser handles nested tags, escaping, and error recovery.

## Validation Checklist

### 4.1 Tag Syntax
- [ ] Verify opening tag: [style] where style is parsed by Style::parse
- [ ] Verify closing tag: [/] closes most recent tag
- [ ] Verify named close: [/bold] closes specific tag
- [ ] Verify escape sequence: backslash-bracket produces literal bracket

### 4.2 ParseElement Enum
- [ ] Verify variants: Text(String), Open(Tag), Close(Option<String>)
- [ ] Verify Tag struct contains: name (String), style (Style)

### 4.3 Parser Behavior
- [ ] Verify render(markup) returns Vec<Segment>
- [ ] Verify nested tags: [bold][red]text[/][/] applies both styles
- [ ] Verify style inheritance: inner tags combine with outer
- [ ] Verify unmatched close tags are treated as literal text
- [ ] Verify unclosed tags at end are auto-closed

### 4.4 Error Handling (MarkupError)
- [ ] Verify InvalidTag for malformed tags
- [ ] Verify UnmatchedClose for close without open
- [ ] Verify error recovery: invalid markup rendered as literal

### 4.5 Complex Cases
- [ ] Test: [bold]a[italic]b[/italic]c[/bold] - proper nesting
- [ ] Test: [red]a[blue]b[/]c[/] - color override then restore
- [ ] Test: [link=URL]click[/] - hyperlink
- [ ] Test: escaped brackets - literal brackets
- [ ] Test: [bold red on blue]text[/] - compound style

## Files to Examine
- src/markup/mod.rs (primary)
- src/style.rs (Style::parse)

-{"id":"rich_rust-shw","title":"Validate Console against RICH_SPEC Section 7","description":"## Objective
Verify the Console implementation in src/console.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 7 exactly.

## Background
Console is the central entry point for rich_rust. It detects terminal capabilities, manages output, and coordinates the rendering pipeline from markup to ANSI codes.

## Validation Checklist

### 7.1 ConsoleOptions Struct
- [ ] Verify fields: max_width, color_system, force_terminal, record, highlight, emoji, safe_box, soft_wrap
- [ ] Verify immutable pattern: options passed to renderables
- [ ] Verify defaults match spec

### 7.2 ConsoleDimensions
- [ ] Verify fields: width, height
- [ ] Verify terminal size detection (fallback to 80x25)
- [ ] Verify COLUMNS/LINES env var override

### 7.3 Console Struct
- [ ] Verify builder pattern: Console::new().width(100).color_system(...)
- [ ] Verify terminal detection: is_terminal(), is_dumb_terminal()
- [ ] Verify color system auto-detection: COLORTERM, TERM env vars
- [ ] Verify force_terminal option bypasses detection

### 7.4 Console Methods
- [ ] Verify print(renderable) outputs to stdout
- [ ] Verify print_styled(text, style) convenience method
- [ ] Verify rule(title) prints horizontal rule
- [ ] Verify clear() clears screen
- [ ] Verify size() returns ConsoleDimensions

### 7.5 Rendering Pipeline
- [ ] Verify: markup parsing -> segment generation -> ANSI encoding
- [ ] Verify style application respects color_system
- [ ] Verify newlines are handled correctly
- [ ] Verify control segments produce correct escape codes

### 7.6 Recording (if implemented)
- [ ] Verify record=true captures output
- [ ] Verify export_text() returns plain text
- [ ] Verify export_html() returns HTML (if implemented)

## Files to Examine
- src/console.rs (primary)
- src/terminal.rs (terminal detection)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 7","acceptance_criteria":"All checklist items verified.  {"id":"rich_rust-t54","title":"expand_widths() ignores column ratios, distributes space equally","description":"## Discrepancy Found

**Location**: src/renderables/table.rs:612-637

**Spec Requirement (RICH_SPEC Section 14.4)**:
Distribute extra space among columns proportionally based on their `ratio` field. Columns with ratio=0 get no extra space.

**Actual Behavior**:
The implementation distributes extra space EQUALLY across all columns, completely ignoring the `ratio` field.

**Test Vector**:
- Input: widths=[20,20,20], ratios=[1,2,1], available=100
- Expected: [30,40,30] (extra 40 distributed 1:2:1)
- Actual: [33,33,34] (extra 40 distributed equally)

**Impact**:
The Column.ratio(n) builder method exists and sets the field, but it has no effect on table layout during expansion.

**Validation Test**:
See tests/e2e_width_algorithms.rs

**Recommended Fix**:
Rewrite expand_widths() to follow Section 14.4 ratio_distribute algorithm.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T13:14:24.140591733-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:31:20.880305518-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:31:20.880305518-05:00","close_reason":"Verified fixed: expand_widths() now correctly uses num_rational::Ratio for ratio-based distribution. All e2e_width_algorithms tests pass (13/13). Test test_expand_widths_with_ratios confirms 1:2:1 ratio produces [30,40,30] as expected.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-t54","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T13:14:39.036581663-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-tkj","title":"Set Up CI/CD Pipeline with Test Artifacts","description":"## Objective
Create CI/CD pipeline that runs all tests and preserves artifacts for debugging.

## Background
CI is essential for catching regressions before merge. The pipeline must run unit tests, E2E tests, and benchmarks, preserving logs and artifacts for debugging failures.

## Implementation Requirements

### GitHub Actions Workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml)
```yaml
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, master]
  pull_request:

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
  RUST_LOG: debug
  RUST_BACKTRACE: 1

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-action@stable
        
      - name: Cache cargo
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/registry
            ~/.cargo/git
            target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
          
      - name: Run unit tests
        run: cargo test --all-features -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee test-output.log
        
      - name: Run E2E tests
        run: cargo test --test '*' -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee e2e-output.log
        
      - name: Upload test logs
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: test-logs
          path: |
            test-output.log
            e2e-output.log
          retention-days: 30
          
  benchmarks:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Run benchmarks
        run: cargo bench -- --noplot 2>&1 | tee bench-output.log
        
      - name: Upload benchmark results
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: benchmark-results
          path: |
            bench-output.log
            target/criterion
          retention-days: 90
          
  coverage:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install cargo-tarpaulin
        run: cargo install cargo-tarpaulin
        
      - name: Generate coverage
        run: cargo tarpaulin --out Xml --output-dir coverage
        
      - name: Upload coverage
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
        with:
          files: coverage/cobertura.xml
```

### Artifact Preservation
- Test output logs (30 days)
- Benchmark results (90 days)
- Coverage reports
- Golden file diffs on failure

### Failure Notifications
- GitHub status checks
- Optional: Slack/email on failure

## Files to Create
- .github/workflows/ci.yml
- .github/workflows/benchmarks.yml (optional separate)","acceptance_criteria":"CI runs on every PR. Artifacts preserved. Coverage tracked.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:47:45.408661933-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:47:45.408661933-05:00","labels":["ci","infrastructure"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-tkj","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-6d6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:48:04.393233497-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
